blurb: Unglamorous but a pollinator powerhouse — the region's #2 plant for specialist native bees: bright yellow daisies cupped in sticky, curled-back resinous bracts on a tough, drought-proof native of hot, lean, disturbed ground. A self-sowing pioneer; deadhead if you want to keep it in bounds.
water: Low; very xeric — a tough pioneer of dry, lean ground
spread: Clumping biennial/short-lived perennial that self-sows readily — a pioneer of disturbed ground; deadhead to limit spread
seasons: Resinous gray-green foliage; yellow daisies cupped in distinctive sticky, curled-back green bracts from midsummer into fall
wildlife: A keystone for specialist native bees (about 68 species — the region's #2 specialist-bee plant) plus many bees, butterflies and beetles; seeds for birds
deer: Resistant — the resinous, bitter foliage is left alone
toxic: Non-toxic to people (the sticky resin was a traditional medicinal); can accumulate selenium on seleniferous soils, a minor livestock note
